full_prompt: Design one representative household concrete biosand filter body compliant with the CAWST Biosand Filter Construction Manual (2022 revision). The filter body is a rectangular concrete box with outer dimensions 305 mm (L) x 305 mm (W) x 900 mm (H), inner cavity 205 mm x 205 mm x 900 mm giving 50 mm wall thickness on all four sides, cast from a 1:2:4 (by volume) ordinary-Portland-cement : sand : aggregate mix with w/c ratio approximately 0.45 and a nominal cube compressive strength of 20 MPa at 28 days. The inside cavity holds (bottom to top): a 50 mm layer of 6-12 mm gravel underdrain around the outlet elbow, a 50 mm layer of 1-6 mm separating gravel, a 500 mm deep layer of fine filter sand (d10 about 0.15-0.20 mm, uniformity coefficient <= 2.5), leaving a supernatant water-column standing-head zone of 50-150 mm (5-15 cm) above the sand surface. The outlet is a 12.7 mm (1/2 in) nominal copper or PVC elbow cast into the wall and rising to an outlet spout at elevation matching the top of the sand bed, so that the standing-head window is defined and the bed is kept saturated between dosings. Design filtration rate is 0.4 m/h (i.e. 0.4 m3 per m2 sand-surface area per hour = 27 mL/min at 205x205 mm area), derived from CAWST operating guidance. Filter must include a concrete lid and a diffuser plate (perforated metal or plastic) installed 50 mm above the top sand level. Deliverable is a CAD model of the concrete filter body including cast-in outlet path and the internal layer labels (not full granular simulation) suitable for (a) linear static structural FEA under hydrostatic + dead load to verify 50 mm wall thickness is adequate, (b) geometric volume check of filter-bed stack-up, (c) Darcy-equivalent hydraulic check of the 0.4 m/h filtration rate against the specified head window. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD of the full filter (beyond Darcy lookup), radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design a household concrete biosand filter body per the CAWST Biosand Filter Construction Manual (2022 revision). The body is a rectangular 1:2:4 cement:sand:aggregate precast box (nominal 50 mm wall, ~20 MPa cube strength) containing a layered underdrain / separating gravel / fine filter sand stack-up with a cast-in outlet elbow rising to set the supernatant standing-head window, plus a concrete lid and diffuser plate. Target filtration rate 0.4 m/h with a 5-15 cm supernatant head.

Envelope: outer 305 x 305 x 900 mm, inner cavity 205 x 205 mm, sand-bed depth 500 mm, diffuser 50 mm above the sand surface.

Load cases.
- LC1 hydrostatic + media: saturated sand + gravel + supernatant, gravity on walls and base.
- LC2 filtration steady: Darcy flow at 0.4 m/h target through the packed sand at a 5-15 cm head (non-FEA Darcy lookup).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Filtration rate 0.4 m/h at 5-15 cm head is a Darcy-law lookup / hand calculation, not CFD.

prompt.title: CAWST concrete biosand filter body
prompt.brief: Design a 305 x 305 x 900 mm concrete biosand filter with 50 mm walls, 1:2:4 mix, filtration 0.4 m/h, head window 5-15 cm.

load_cases.name: hydrostatic_plus_media
load_cases.application: saturated sand + gravel + supernatant, gravity load on walls and base

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Full CFD not required; Darcy analytical lookup is sufficient and out of FEA scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Thick short concrete walls; no buckling.

eval_coverage: CAWST 1:2:4 concrete mix as design constraint
